mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL indicates rising consumer prices that erode real corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward test shows positive mean 20-day SPX return following month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases, or Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month increase in UNRATE of at least 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating household income prospects and reduced consumption forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal's Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS indicate a restrictive monetary stance amplifying inflation-driven margin compression, which jointly predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS does not produce negative average 20-day SPX returns, or the interaction term's incremental R-squared is below 1% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, month-over-month declines in RSAFS signal weakening consumer demand that directly reduces top-line revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where CPIAUCSL is above its 12-month rolling average, declines in RSAFS are followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO reflect expanding industrial capacity utilization and higher corporate operating leverage, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive monthly changes in INDPRO are followed by negative mean 5-day SPX returns over a 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in HOUST exceeding 50 thousand units signal accelerating residential investment and construction employment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases above 50k are followed by negative average 60-day SPX returns in more than half of triggered instances, or Rank IC is not positive (p > 0.05) over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in PAYEMS and declines in UNRATE signal tightening labor markets that boost wage income and consumer spending, jointly predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to produce positive average 20-day SPX returns, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (t-stat < 2.0) in walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS level above 5.0 percent indicates restrictive monetary policy that raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations, or the signal's Rank IC is above -0.02 over a 2-year rolling window.
